name: Rack Echo
explanation: A pristine 80s studio rack digital delay with a doubler mode. Clean, hi-fi repeats (no tape grit, no companding) run through a low-pass filtered feedback path so successive echoes gently roll off their highs. A subtle stereo LFO modulates the read position so a single repeat can be fattened into a wide chorused double or slapback. Controls: Time, Feedback, Modulation (doubler/chorus depth), Tone (repeat HF) and dry/wet Mix; Mix at 0 is dry.
